paragraph-intro: "My work starts with the body — specifically, with everything the body is told to hide. Raised in Canada by Uruguayan parents, I learned early that culture shapes how we express and relate to our bodies, and that unlearning those limits offers the freedom to choose. For twenty-five years, as a performer, choreographer, filmmaker, and somatic practitioner, I've been making space for sensuality, queerness, and intimacy to be examined honestly and without shame. That work has taken many forms: internationally touring performances, queer films circling the globe, a thriving somatic practice, and a ballroom community built from the ground up in Montréal."
paragraph-chouinard:
GERARD X REYES is a queer, multidisciplinary Uruguayan-Canadian artist (they/he) based in Montréal/Tiohtià:ke. As a choreographer, dancer, filmmaker, Certified Somatic Sexologist, and intimacy coordinator, their work moves across performance, installation, film, and somatic practice — always at the intersection of sensuality, queerness, and the body as a site of knowledge.

Following seven years as a company member of the internationally acclaimed Compagnie Marie Chouinard (2006–2013) — during which they performed eight repertory works across Europe, Asia, and the Americas and received a Gemini Award for Best Performance in Chouinard's film bODY_rEMIX — Reyes returned to New York to train with voguing legends including Leiomy Maldonado, Jose Xtravaganza, Benny Ninja, and Godfather Archie Burnett Ninja. This research culminated in their critically acclaimed debut solo The Principle of Pleasure (2015), which played to sold-out audiences at Festival TransAmériques (Montréal), SummerWorks (Toronto), Tanzfabrik (Berlin), and Sziget Music Festival (Budapest), among others, touring internationally from 2015 to 2023.
paragraph-pop:
Their 5-part multimedia series Public / Private Parts (2022–2025) explores a spectrum of queer intimacy and raw embodiment in an era where eroticism is performed and flattened to 2D screens. Developed in collaboration with queer sex workers and somatic educators in Berlin — during which Reyes also completed their certification as a Somatic Sexologist — the series encompasses a live performance-video installation (Festival TransAmériques, Usine C, Parcours Danse), two short films (ECSTATIC UNITY and The Key to Dreams), a documentary interview collection, and a podcast interrogating moral censorship, pornography, and the urgent need for erotic education. Their debut short films have toured to 37 festivals internationally — Chéries-Chéris (Paris), Fringe! Queer Film and Arts (London), BLUSH (San Francisco), Xposed (Berlin), FID:RIO (Rio de Janeiro), and beyond.

In 2023, Reyes was commissioned as the choreographer and movement coach for rising Québécoise filmmaker Sophie Dupuis' feature film SOLO, which received the Best Canadian Feature Film award at TIFF. That same year, they appeared as guest performer in acclaimed theatre director Philip Cyr's production of Martin Crimp's Atteintes à sa vie.
paragraph-ballroom:
Between 2013 and 2019, Reyes was a founding force in Montréal's Kiki Ballroom scene — teaching voguing, mentoring emerging 2SLGBTQ+ artists, and producing balls for Fierté Montréal and Pride Canada, bringing icons including Mike Q, Precious, Twiggy Pucci Garçon, and Chi Chi Mizrahi to the city for the first time.

Reyes' most recent research has taken them beyond the city and the stage — guided by field researcher Jak Maliszewski, they spent time in sanctuaries and various ecosystems across Peru, Namibia, Belize, and Australia, caring for and observing animals, and learning from communities whose relationships to land, body, and kinship have not been severed in the ways dominant urban culture demands.
paragraph-bodywork: Reyes' new performance work The Inverted Kingdom is rooted in this research. Inspired by Uruguayan artist Joaquín Torres-García's painting Inverted America, Reyes is excavating real stories from their paternal family tree, as well as the hidden queer and Afro-Uruguayan roots of tango by studying it as a social and listening practice. Creation residencies are planned in Montevideo in 2026–27 in collaboration with Uruguayan tango artist Rodrigo Fleitas and institutional partners including the Museo Nacional de Artes Visuales (MNAV), El Instituto Nacional de Artes Escénicas (INAE), and Espacio de Arte Contemporáneo (EAC).
text-pop-1: MORE ON The Principle of Pleasure
